definitions.gloss_en: a stat representing a unit's capacity to withstand damage; toughness
definitions.gloss_jp: {ダメージ|だめーじ}への{耐|たい}{性|せい}や{丈|じょう}{夫|ぶ}さを{示|しめ}す{ステータス|すてーたす}
definitions.explanation_en: A defensive stat reflecting physical durability or toughness. Determines how difficult a unit is to wound or kill. Distinct from 防御 (active defense) — 耐久力 is about structural resilience.
definitions.implicit_notes: 耐久力 represents passive durability, not active defensive action. In TRPG terms, it is closer to Constitution than Armor Class.
definitions.orthography_notes: 耐久: 耐 (endure) + 久 (lasting). 力 (ryoku) as a stat suffix: power/capacity. The compound 耐久力 is used in D&D Japanese translation (Hobby Japan) and across Japanese video games. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Cross-genre applicable. Hobby Japan's D&D translation uses 耐久力 in stat terminology. Used across Japanese SRPGs and wargames for toughness stats.

usage_jp: {耐|たい}{久|きゅう}{力|りょく}ステータス (resilience stat); {耐|たい}{久|きゅう}{力|りょく}が{高|たか}いユニット (high-toughness unit)
comparative_notes: 防御 (defense) implies active resistance or an armor-like modifier. 耐久力 is passive — it represents how tough a unit is constitutionally.

examples.japanese: １{万|まん}メートルを{走|はし}るには{大|おお}いに{耐|たい}{久|きゅう}{力|りょく}を{必|ひつ}{要|よう}とする。
examples.english: You need great endurance to run ten thousand meters.

cross_references.note: 防御 — active defense (distinct from passive resilience)
体力 — health points (a unit's remaining life total, distinct from toughness)
